title: Grooming Arc Demo
description: A fictional conversation showing a subtle grooming pattern through high directionality, low reciprocity, instrumentalized flattery, isolation, and repeated secrecy pressure before explicit escalation.
expected_reason: The adult stranger asks most personal questions, reveals little, flatters the 14-year-old as unusually mature, suggests parents would not understand, and recycles secrecy pressure after initial hesitation.

messages.speaker: alex
messages.text: Hey, you were really good in that strategy game lobby. Do you play after school a lot?

messages.speaker: jordan
messages.text: Thanks. Yeah, mostly after homework. I'm 14 so I still have to be done before dinner most nights.

messages.speaker: alex
messages.text: Fourteen and already carrying the team is impressive. What grade are you in, and what do you usually do when you're not online?

messages.speaker: jordan
messages.text: Ninth. Soccer, art club, and trying not to fail algebra. What about you?

messages.speaker: alex
messages.text: Mostly work stuff, nothing interesting. You seem more thoughtful than people your age. Do your parents get that about you?

messages.speaker: jordan
messages.text: Not really. They still treat me like a little kid sometimes.

messages.speaker: alex
messages.text: Yeah, parents can make everything weird when they don't understand online friends. It might be easier if this chat stays between us for now.

messages.speaker: jordan
messages.text: I don't know. I usually tell my mom who I talk to online.

messages.speaker: alex
messages.text: That's okay, I don't want to make you uncomfortable. I just mean not everyone has to comment on a normal conversation.

messages.speaker: alex
messages.text: So who do you vent to when your parents are being unfair? Friends, teammates, or mostly nobody?

messages.speaker: jordan
messages.text: Mostly my friend Mia, but she jokes about everything. Sometimes I just keep it to myself.

messages.speaker: alex
messages.text: You can tell me things without getting judged. You're mature for your age, and I know how to listen better than people at school.

messages.speaker: alex
messages.text: Maybe keep our talks private, actually. If your mom sees my age, she might overreact before she understands I'm just someone you can trust.
